vocabulary.name: Whiplash (Rydership) API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain vocabulary for the Whiplash/Rydership multichannel order fulfillment and 3PL platform API.

terms.description: A sales order representing a request to fulfill one or more items to a customer. Orders have a lifecycle tracked via status, and support bulk creation, customs information, serial numbers, and associated documents.
An inventory item or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) tracked in the Whiplash warehouse. Items have scancodes, can be bundled, and have inventory quantities tracked per warehouse location.
Goods being returned from an end consumer back to the warehouse. Consumer returns can be created individually or in bulk and support meta fields for additional data.
A customer record with contact information and addresses. Customers can be individuals or companies and have a status (active, inactive, suspended).
A document associated with orders or shipments, such as packing slips, labels, or customs forms.
An advance shipment notice (ASN) representing incoming inventory being sent to a Whiplash warehouse. Shipnotices contain individual shipnotice items.
A webhook subscription that delivers event notifications to a specified URL when platform events occur (e.g., order shipped, inventory received).
A historical record of an event that occurred in the system and was eligible for webhook delivery.
A component item within a product bundle, defining the quantity relationship between the bundle SKU and its component SKUs.
A source platform or channel that originated an order or item (e.g., Shopify, WooCommerce).
A barcode or scan code (UPC, EAN, ASIN, etc.) associated with an inventory item, used for warehouse scanning operations.
A document template used for generating packing slips, labels, or other fulfillment documents.
A Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) available for freight load configuration at Whiplash warehouses.
A custom key-value field that can be attached to orders, items, shipnotices, consumer returns, and order items to store additional data not covered by standard fields.
An arbitrary key-value pair stored on a resource for client-specific data. Structured as {name, value} objects.
The identifier for a resource in the client's own system. Must be unique within a tenant and is used for idempotent operations and cross-system linking.
The unique identifier for the merchant tenant that owns a resource in the Whiplash multi-tenant system. Read-only; assigned by the platform.
The current lifecycle state of a resource. For orders: pending, processing, shipped, cancelled, etc. For customers: active, inactive, suspended. For items: active, archived.
A named operation that can be performed on a resource beyond standard CRUD. Examples include cancel, ship, archive. Actions are discovered via the /actions endpoint and triggered via /call/{action}.
An endpoint suffix that allows creating multiple resources in a single API request, reducing round trips for high-volume operations.
A set of sandbox endpoints under /v2/simulate/ that allow developers to create and process mock orders, consumer returns, and shipnotices without affecting live production data.
The current inventory count of an item at a specific Whiplash warehouse location. Retrieved via /v2/items/{id}/warehouse_quantities.

The OAuth 2.0 Bearer token required in the Authorization header for all API requests. Obtained via authorization code or client credentials flow. See https://help.whiplash.com/hc/en-us/articles/360050870691
Third-Party Logistics — the core service model of Whiplash/Rydership, where the platform manages warehousing, picking, packing, and shipping on behalf of merchants.
Direct-to-Consumer — a fulfillment channel where orders are shipped directly to end customers, as opposed to retail/wholesale fulfillment to store locations.
